title: Intensely Sour Middle Eastern Chicken Curry
description: A light and sour Middle Eastern chicken curry featuring coconut cream, perfect for the monsoon season, and made nut-free-friendly by using sunflower seeds as a thickening agent and spherification for a unique texture.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the chicken breast pieces and cook until browned on all sides.
Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.
Add the sunflower seeds to the pan and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the lemon juice, apple cider vinegar, and coconut cream to the pan, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 5 minutes, or until slightly thickened.
Stir in the sodium alginate and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the chicken back to the pan and stir to coat with the curry sauce.
Using a syringe or spoon, drop small amounts of the curry sauce into a bath of cold water to create spheres.
Serve the chicken and curry sauce spheres hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
